---
title: "Installing other versions"
---

This guide explains how to install a specific version of Ribasim, such as a pre-release (release candidate) or an older stable release.
For installing the latest stable release, see the [Installation](/getting-started/install.qmd) page.

All available versions can be found on the [Ribasim releases page on GitHub](https://github.com/Deltares/Ribasim/releases).

::: {.callout-important}
**All Ribasim component versions must be the same!**

The Ribasim core, Ribasim Python package, and Ribasim QGIS plugin must all be of the same version.
Running with mismatched versions will likely fail.
:::

# Choosing a version

Ribasim uses the following versioning scheme:

- **Releases** have version numbers like `2025.6.0`.
- **Pre-releases** (release candidates) have version numbers like `2026.1.0-rc1`. These are development versions used for testing before a stable release.

Browse the [GitHub releases page](https://github.com/Deltares/Ribasim/releases) to see all available versions.
Pre-releases are marked with a "Pre-release" label.

# Install a specific Ribasim core version {#sec-core}

## Windows installation script

The `install.ps1` script always installs the latest stable release.
To install a specific version, use the manual installation method below.

## Manual installation

Download the appropriate zip file for your operating system from the [GitHub releases page](https://github.com/Deltares/Ribasim/releases).
Navigate to the desired version and download:

- `ribasim_windows.zip` for Windows
- `ribasim_linux.zip` for Linux

For example, to install version `2026.1.0-rc1`, go to the [v2026.1.0-rc1 release](https://github.com/Deltares/Ribasim/releases/tag/v2026.1.0-rc1) and download the zip file from there.

Then follow the [manual installation instructions](/getting-started/install.qmd#sec-install-core) to extract and set up the binary.

# Install a specific Ribasim Python version {#sec-python}

Replace the version number in the install command with the desired version.
If you don't have a project set up yet, follow the initialization steps on the [Installation](/getting-started/install.qmd#sec-install-python) page first.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## uv

```sh
uv add ribasim==2026.1.0rc1
```

## pixi

```sh
pixi add --pypi ribasim==2026.1.0rc1
```

## pip

```sh
pip install --upgrade ribasim==2026.1.0rc1
```

:::

::: {.callout-note}
Pre-release versions use a slightly different format in Python package managers: `2026.1.0rc1` instead of `2026.1.0-rc1` (no hyphen).
This follows the [PEP 440](https://peps.python.org/pep-0440/) versioning standard.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
Pre-releases are only published to [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/ribasim/), not to conda-forge.
Therefore `conda install` cannot be used for pre-release versions.
For stable releases, all package managers (including conda) work as described on the [Installation](/getting-started/install.qmd#sec-install-python) page.
:::

# Install a specific Ribasim QGIS plugin version {#sec-qgis}

The QGIS Plugin Repository installs the latest stable release of the Ribasim QGIS plugin.
To install a specific version, use the ZIP file method:

1. Go to the [GitHub releases page](https://github.com/Deltares/Ribasim/releases) and navigate to the desired version.
2. Download `ribasim_qgis.zip` from the release assets.
3. In QGIS, go to *Plugins* > *Manage and Install Plugins...* > *Install from ZIP*.
4. Browse to the downloaded `ribasim_qgis.zip` and click *Install Plugin*.

See the [QGIS plugin installation instructions](/getting-started/install.qmd#sec-install-qgis-plugin) for more details on installing from ZIP.
